steps.title: Find the smaller map's side length
steps.content: "70% shorter" means the new side is \(100\%-70\%=30\%\) of the original:$$45\times 0.30=13.5\text{ inches}.$$

steps.title: Total real distance the side represents
steps.content: The original map's side spans$$45\text{ in}\times 13\text{ mi/in}=585\text{ miles},$$and the smaller map shows the same region, so its 13.5-inch side still represents 585 miles.

steps.title: Compute miles per inch on the smaller map
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) \(3.90\): would need \(585/150\) — way too small.
  • B) \(7.65\): doesn't match \(585/13.5\).
  • C) \(31.50\): mixes up the scaling — doesn't equal \(585/13.5\).
  • D) \(43.33\): equals \(585/13.5=130/3\).
That matches choice D — D is correct.
